note: Media Library Edit is a hook-only module with no routes, permissions, services, or plugins; its whole surface is third-party widget settings on the core media_library_widget plus AJAX/form-mode runtime behavior. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded), probing facts an agent would otherwise read the .module and schema to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the media_library_edit module enabled, where do I turn on the edit button for a media reference field? There is no admin settings page for the module.
evals.expected_output: It has no config route; you enable it in the field's Manage form display, in the settings of the Media library (media_library_widget) widget, via its third-party settings.

evals.prompt: Which two settings does media_library_edit add to the Media library widget, and what config schema type stores them?
evals.expected_output: It adds show_edit (checkbox 'Show edit button') and edit_form_mode (select 'Form mode'), stored under the config schema type field.widget.third_party.media_library_edit.

evals.prompt: Does media_library_edit add an edit button to any file/media widget? Which widget plugin id does it target?
evals.expected_output: Only the core Media library widget, plugin id media_library_widget; the alter hooks check for that plugin id and do nothing for other widgets.

evals.prompt: media_library_edit defines no permissions. What determines whether a given editor sees the edit button on a selected media item?
evals.expected_output: There are no module permissions; the button only renders when the media item's access('update') is TRUE and the media entity type has an 'edit-form' link template.

evals.prompt: How does media_library_edit make the edit modal render a specific media form mode instead of the default? Name the query parameter and the hook involved.
evals.expected_output: The edit URL carries a media_library_form_mode=<mode> query param; hook_entity_form_display_alter swaps the form display to that form mode when it is valid for the bundle.

evals.prompt: When you save the media edit form inside media_library_edit's modal, what happens to the delete action and how does the item update without a full page reload?
evals.expected_output: hook_form_alter (when media_library_edit=ajax) hides the delete action and rewires save to the AJAX callback _media_library_edit_media_edit_update, which closes the dialog and ReplaceCommands the item's rendered media_library view.

evals.prompt: Are media_library_edit's per-widget settings deployable as configuration, and does the module define its own settings config object?
evals.expected_output: It defines no standalone settings object; the settings live as third-party settings on the entity form display config, so they export/deploy with drush config:export like the rest of the form display.
